title: Overnight Session-Return Decomposition / Overnight Drift -- FROZEN strategy specification (spec only)

provenance.head_note: HEAD is exactly the S30-D1 commit 9e1c565; factory tree clean except the known untracked reports/crypto_d1_research_lane_protocol/ folder (untouched here); nothing staged; no automation-touched factory file since S30-D1.

2_market_timeframe.independent_confirmation_later: ES continuous futures, daily bars (separate later step)
2_market_timeframe.out_of_sample: 2023-2025 -- SEALED until the OOS protocol is pre-registered and committed
2_market_timeframe.micros_note: MNQ/MES are same-underlying proxies, descriptive only; never independent confirmation.

3_core_hypothesis: A systematic overnight risk premium may exist: the return from prior close to next open (overnight leg) may differ from, and be more reliably positive than, the open-to-close day-session leg. The edge, if real, must come from MANY small repeated overnight observations, not from a few large trend captures. This is the structural antidote to the failure that parked every prior branch (small-N directional triggers whose net hinged on 3 fat-tail winners and failed the top-3-removal gate): an every-session overnight harvest is the average of thousands of independent daily bets and cannot be dominated by a handful of days -- a property that is itself a pre-registered gate.

4_measurement_definition.overnight_return: open[t] - close[t-1]  (the leg harvested in v1)
4_measurement_definition.total_day_return: close[t] - close[t-1]  (= overnight + day_session by construction)
4_measurement_definition.measurement_validity_is_a_HARD_GATE: Daily futures bars may use session-specific open/close conventions; the daily open/close prints may NOT cleanly bracket the intended overnight (prior-settle -> next-open) window.
required_checks_before_any_edge_claim: S30-D3/D4 must verify the offline daily open/close for NQ (and ES) actually represent the overnight/day boundary the hypothesis assumes, e.g. overnight + day_session reconstructs total_day_return exactly, and open/close correspond to a consistent session convention across the entire 2013-2022 window.

If the daily bar open/close cannot be trusted to represent the overnight/day split, the branch MUST STOP and either be re-scoped as requiring intraday/session-level data we do not have offline, or be parked. No edge claim may be made on a mis-measured leg.

5_direction_v1.definition: Enter long at close[t-1], exit at open[t]. Hold the overnight leg only.
5_direction_v1.day_session_exposure: NONE in v1 (open->close leg is measured for comparison per section 11 but never traded in v1).
5_direction_v1.why_long_only_overnight: Cleanest single expression of the risk-premium hypothesis; matches the documented direction of the equity-index overnight anomaly; fades nothing; stacks no filters; keeps the first frozen test unambiguous. A short or day-session variant is a SEPARATE later branch, not a v1 tweak.

6_eligibility_filters.v1_rule: ALL valid trading days eligible. No trend filter. No RSI/EMA filter. No volatility filter -- tests the raw overnight effect directly and maximizes observation count (attacks the S27/S28 low-count failure).
6_eligibility_filters.eligible_day: close[t-1] and open[t] both present, finite, and > 0; t-1 and t are consecutive valid trading sessions (no gap across a data hole or holiday boundary that breaks the prior-close -> next-open chain).
6_eligibility_filters.excluded: missing/invalid/zero open or close; the first bar of the dataset (no t-1); any bar where session-consecutiveness fails.
6_eligibility_filters.considered_and_rejected_v1: A volatility guard (e.g. skip when ATR20[t-1] is extreme) -- rejected to avoid a free parameter / overfit surface; any such guard is a NEW branch, not a v1 option.

7_entry_exit_timing.no_lookahead: entry uses the known prior close; exit is the next session open.
7_entry_exit_timing.no_intraday_stop_or_target: daily bars cannot observe the overnight path, so an intra-overnight stop/target would be fabricated. Position held flat from prior close to next open with NO path-dependent exit.

8_accounting_method.rationale: Overnight drift is small and frequent; an R-based stop/target does not fit v1 (there is no stop). Accounting is distribution-based.
8_accounting_method.primary_metric: raw per-day overnight return in points and in percent ((open[t]-close[t-1])/close[t-1]).
8_accounting_method.secondary_normalized_metric: normalized_return = (open[t]-close[t-1]) / ATR20[t-1]  (Wilder ATR over 20 daily bars, computed on data through t-1 only -- no lookahead).
8_accounting_method.no_artificial_stop_target: Do NOT force an artificial stop/target if the daily data cannot support it.

9_cost_friction.round_trip: one round trip per day (enter at prior close, exit at next open).
9_cost_friction.pre_register_units: per-round-trip cost expressed in points, in percent, and as a fraction of ATR20[t-1].
9_cost_friction.freeze_note: exact point/percent values to be frozen in S30-D3 engine constants before any run, then never changed.
9_cost_friction.hard_rule: If the expected per-day overnight edge is smaller than realistic friction, the branch FAILS. A frequent tiny edge that does not clear cost is not a strategy (the S26 FRICTION_SENSITIVE lesson applied up front).

10_validation_ladder_plan.note: Pre-registered S30 sub-step plan using the completed validation factory (Factory-D2..D11). No step is run now.
10_validation_ladder_plan.S30-D3: engine + tests only (frozen rules; no run) -- INCLUDES the section-4 measurement-validity check as code.
10_validation_ladder_plan.S30-D4: IS baseline (2013-2022) via validation_is_runner; hard 2023-2025 seal; + measurement-validity gate report.
10_validation_ladder_plan.S30-D5: OOS protocol pre-registration via validation_oos_runner; COMMIT before any OOS.
10_validation_ladder_plan.S30-D6: OOS run ONCE (2023-2025) against the committed protocol.
10_validation_ladder_plan.S30-D7: distribution / overnight-significance via validation_entry_significance (adapted per section 11).
10_validation_ladder_plan.S30-D8: sequence risk / bootstrap + top-day dependence via validation_sequence_risk.
10_validation_ladder_plan.S30-D12: friction stress at low/moderate/high/severe via validation_friction.
10_validation_ladder_plan.S30-D13: final decision / readiness gate via validation_decision.

11_significance_adaptation.note: This is a daily calendar exposure, not sparse discrete entries, so entry-significance is adapted to a DISTRIBUTION test.
11_significance_adaptation.test_A_vs_zero: overnight returns vs zero (is the mean/median overnight return reliably positive?).
11_significance_adaptation.test_B_vs_day_session: overnight returns vs day-session returns (is the overnight leg reliably different from / better than the open->close leg? -- isolates WHEN the premium accrues).
11_significance_adaptation.test_C_vs_random: real overnight sequence vs a sign-flip / permutation / bootstrap baseline (is the observed mean beyond random sign/order?).
11_significance_adaptation.no_calendar_cherry_picking: No cherry-picking weekdays or months in v1. Any weekday/month/turn-of-month conditioning is a SEPARATE pre-registered branch, never added to v1 after seeing results.

12_pass_watch_fail_gates.measurement_validity: PASS open/close cleanly reconstruct overnight/day split on full IS | WATCH minor reconcilable gaps | FAIL split not representable -> STOP/PARK
12_pass_watch_fail_gates.is_observation_count: PASS thousands (~every eligible day 2013-2022) | WATCH hundreds | FAIL dozens (a measurement bug)
12_pass_watch_fail_gates.distribution_health: PASS positive median or acceptable skew, not one-sided tail | WATCH borderline | FAIL median <= 0 with mean propped by tail
12_pass_watch_fail_gates.gain_loss_or_win_rate: PASS favorable & reported | WATCH borderline | FAIL unfavorable
12_pass_watch_fail_gates.net_after_fixed_friction: PASS positive at moderate cost | WATCH positive only at low cost | FAIL negative at moderate cost
12_pass_watch_fail_gates.top_day_fat_tail_dependence: PASS net stays positive after removing top 1% of days AND top 3 days | WATCH thin without them | FAIL net flips negative ex-top-days (the universal parked-branch killer)
12_pass_watch_fail_gates.oos: PASS positive after friction | WATCH positive only pre-friction | FAIL negative after friction
12_pass_watch_fail_gates.es_corroboration: PASS same direction & sign on ES | WATCH weak/mixed | FAIL ES contradicts NQ
12_pass_watch_fail_gates.walk_forward: PASS edge present across multiple eras | WATCH concentrated | FAIL only one era carries the result

13_anti_overfit_rules: No adding weekday / month / turn-of-month filters after any result (each is a new branch).
No switching from the overnight leg to the day-session leg after seeing results.
No changing atr_period (20) or the frozen friction levels after any result.
No parameter sweeps; no grid search; no 'best of N'.
No OOS run before the OOS protocol is pre-registered AND committed.
No paper/live promotion from the spec or from IS results; paper/live requires a separate readiness memo with explicit override fields.
Any rule change opens a NEW branch -- S30 is never tuned in place.
14_main_expected_failure_modes: Measurement failure: daily bar open/close do not represent the desired session boundary (the binding section-4 gate) -> any edge is an artifact.
Friction failure: the per-day edge is too small to clear realistic cost (the S26 failure).
Regime concentration: edge concentrated in crisis / high-vol years rather than durable across 2013-2022.
Tail risk: overnight gaps create fat-tail losses, and/or the apparent edge is itself a few extreme up-gaps (the top-day-dependence gate).
No multi-market corroboration: ES does not confirm NQ's direction.
Distribution fragility: the mean depends on a handful of extreme days (the inverse risk to the count advantage).
Calendar/holiday artifacts: futures session calendar / holiday handling breaks the prior-close -> next-open chain and injects spurious 'overnight' returns.
15_final_line: S30-D2 is a frozen hypothesis specification only; no strategy code, backtest, OOS, optimization, paper trading, or live trading is authorized.
trading_recommendation: NONE. Frozen specification only. Donchian, S26, S27, S28, S29 remain PARKED; no active strategy code; no paper/live system exists or is authorized. The frozen S30 overnight-drift rules are a research hypothesis to be tested under the factory ladder -- gated first on measurement validity and friction -- not a trade.
